layman_headline: The crypto market is bleeding — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: Bitcoin dropped below $62,200 amid geopolitical fears around the Strait of Hormuz and a broad risk-asset selloff. Fear dominates the market at 28/100, and almost every altcoin is deep in the red. While fear can create buying opportunities, none of the current candidates show strong enough technical setups to justify entering now. The best approach is to wait for the selloff to stabilize before committing capital.
layman_action: Stay in cash and watch for signs of stabilization. Do not chase any of the bouncing coins — wait for confirmed support holds and improving momentum before entering new positions.
expert_reasoning: Surveying all seven candidates reveals no high-conviction entry. KITE and BONK have deeply bearish chart structures (-1.4 bias). GRASS dropped 33% in a week but shows a Bearish Engulfing with terrible R/R (0.48x). LIT is overbought at RSI 72. VELVET surged 22% today but has a bearish chart bias and 0.26x R/R. ETHFI lacks chart data entirely. MON is the most interesting with RSI 41, a Bullish Marubozu, and neutral chart bias, but price remains below all MAs in a downtrend with only 0.46x risk/reward. With BTC dominance at 56% crushing alts and Fear at 28, the environment strongly favors patience. Our signal credibility data shows Fear_Greed at 80% win rate — but that works best when combined with a confirmed technical setup, which we don't have yet.
risk_factors: Trump's Hormuz Strait rhetoric could escalate geopolitical risk further, driving another leg down in crypto
BTC dominance at 56% means capital is flowing away from alts — any alt position faces structural headwinds
None of the candidates show confirmed support holds or bullish technical confirmations
watch_list: BTC price action around $60,000 psychological support — a break below would intensify alt selloff
MON holding $0.0185 support and whether it can reclaim its 20-day SMA
Fear & Greed index trajectory — look for a sustained move above 30 to signal sentiment bottom
market_context: Broad risk-off environment with BTC down 3.2% to $62,126, geopolitical threats around Hormuz Strait, and Fear & Greed at 28. BTC dominance at 56% is squeezing altcoins hard — this is not an environment for aggressive alt entries.
context_banner: Market fear is elevated but not yet at capitulation levels — the smart play is to prepare watchlists and wait for confirmed stabilization before deploying capital.

key_signals.description: Fear at 28/100 with improving trend (up from 24.6 weekly avg) suggests we may be approaching a sentiment bottom, but not there yet — historically an 80% reliable signal when combined with technical confirmation.
Bitcoin panic-selling may be ending as seller profit margins disappear — a potential early signal of exhaustion, but needs price confirmation.
Trump's Hormuz Strait comments triggered a risk-asset rout pushing BTC toward $62K — geopolitical uncertainty is the dominant near-term headwind.
MON RSI at 41 and BONK at 40 are approaching oversold but haven't reached the sub-30 levels that historically produce the strongest bounces.
No candidate shows a bullish chart bias above +0.4 and most have bearish structures below all moving averages — the technical landscape does not support new entries.
